info.name: Dine Sync API
info.description:
QR-based smart restaurant ordering platform — full API surface across the gateway and all 8 microservices (auth, restaurant, menu, order, kitchen-queue, payment, inventory, notification).

**Setup**: import the companion `DineSync.postman_environment.json`, select it, then run requests top-to-bottom within `00 - Health Checks` → `01 - Auth Service` → `02 - Restaurant Service` → ... . Most requests chain off environment variables captured by the previous request's test script (e.g. `owner_access_token`, `restaurant_id`, `table_id`, `order_id`) — running a folder in order (Runner or Ctrl/Cmd+click 'Run folder') exercises a realistic end-to-end flow.

**Auth model**: the gateway verifies the JWT and forwards `X-User-ID`/`X-User-Role`/`X-Restaurant-ID` to services; services also accept a direct `Authorization: Bearer <token>` when hit standalone. All requests in this collection go through the gateway (`{{base_url}}`, default `http://localhost:8000`).

**Roles**: owner, floor_manager, chef, customer. Staff (floor_manager/chef) accounts are provisioned via the invite flow in `02 - Restaurant Service > Staff`, not direct registration.

item.name: 00 - Health Checks
item.description: Gateway health via {{base_url}}, plus each service's own direct health endpoint (useful when diagnosing which container is down).

item.name: 01 - Auth Service
item.description: Registration, login, token refresh, and session management. Routed publicly through the gateway at /api/v1/auth (auth-service owns JWT issuance).

request.description: Creates a user and returns tokens. role: owner | floor_manager | chef | customer (defaults to customer). In production, staff accounts are normally created via the invite flow (see Restaurant Service > Staff), not direct registration.
Optional — customers can order anonymously; logging in just attaches customerId to their orders.
Authenticates a user and returns/sets access + refresh tokens.
Issues a new access token. Refresh token may also come from the HTTP-only cookie.
Returns the authenticated user's profile.
Deletes stored refresh tokens for the authenticated user and clears auth cookies.

item.name: 02 - Restaurant Service
item.description: Restaurants, tables, and staff. Owner/floor_manager scoped, tenant-isolated by restaurantId.

request.description: Creates a restaurant for the authenticated owner.
Returns the authenticated owner's active restaurant with tables and staff.
Any authenticated user can call this. Owners/staff of the restaurant get the full record (tables + staff); everyone else gets a restricted public-safe subset.

item.name: Tables
item.description: Table + QR token management. Requires X-Restaurant-ID tenant header or restaurantId claim on the token.

request.description: Creates a table and generates a unique QR token.
Soft delete — sets isActive: false.

item.name: Staff
item.description: Invite-based staff onboarding. inviteStaff sends an email with a JWT invite link — the raw token isn't returned by the API for security, so `Accept Invite` below needs {{staff_invite_token}} pasted in manually from the email/server log when testing end-to-end.

request.description: role: floor_manager | chef. Sends an invite email valid for 48h.
Public — validates the invite token and returns { email, role, restaurantId } for the accept form to prefill.
Public — staff member completes registration and is linked to the restaurant. Returns tokens.
Soft delete — sets isActive: false.

item.name: 03 - Menu Service
item.description: Menu catalog: categories, dishes, customizations, add-ons, and the public QR-scan menu.

item.name: Categories
item.description: Menu categories (owner manages, floor_manager can view).

request.description: Soft delete.
owner or floor_manager.
Soft delete.

item.name: Public Menu (QR scan)
item.description: No auth — hit directly by the customer-facing menu page after a QR scan.

request.description: Full menu grouped by category, available dishes only.

item.name: 04 - Order Service
item.description: Order placement/tracking (public, for anonymous QR customers) and order lifecycle management (owner/floor_manager/chef).

request.description: Public — customer places an order from the QR page. Optionally send Authorization: Bearer <customer_access_token> to attach the order to a logged-in customer.
Public — customer polls this to see their table's order status.
owner or floor_manager.
owner, floor_manager, or chef.
status transitions: placed→approved/cancelled, approved→in_progress/cancelled, in_progress→ready, ready→served. Role: floor_manager, chef, or owner.

item.name: 05 - Kitchen Queue Service
item.description: Kitchen prep queue, populated from order-service Kafka events. chef/floor_manager only.

request.description: Tickets with status queued/preparing/ready, oldest first.
status transitions: queued→preparing/cancelled, preparing→ready/cancelled, ready→served.

item.name: 06 - Payment Service
item.description: Checkout + payment confirmation. Uses a swappable provider (mock by default — see PAYMENT_PROVIDER env var). The webhook request below signs its own body with the mock provider's HMAC secret so it can be run standalone against a mock-provider deployment.

request.description: Any authenticated role. Creates a Payment row + a provider order for the checkout widget.

Public — this is what the payment provider calls back. Only works against the `mock` provider (PAYMENT_PROVIDER=mock, the default) using MOCK_WEBHOOK_SECRET set below. The pre-request script recomputes the body string and its HMAC-SHA256 signature so they always match byte-for-byte; set the {{mock_webhook_secret}} environment variable to the same value as payment-service's MOCK_WEBHOOK_SECRET before running. Publishes payment.confirmed over Kafka, which order-service consumes to mark the order paid.

exec: payload: { payment: { entity: { id: paymentId, order_id: pm.environment.get('provider_order_id'), status: 'captured' } } },

item.name: 07 - Inventory Service
item.description: Per-dish stock tracking, auto-decremented when order-service publishes order.approved. owner/floor_manager only.

request.description: Includes the 20 most recent stock logs.
reason: restock | manual_adjustment | correction. change can be negative.
Soft delete — sets isTracked: false.

item.name: 08 - Notification Service
item.description: Notification history (REST) + real-time push (Socket.IO, not representable in a REST collection — connect a Socket.IO client to {{notification_service_url}} with auth: { token: <accessToken> } and listen for the 'notification' event).
